zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir David Hay of Lochorwart and Yester於大約1394年出生在英國蘇格蘭米德洛迪安時，他的父親Sir William Hay, Laird of Locherworth and Yester20歲，母親Alice de la Haye13歲。他於1425年與Elizabeth Mary Douglas of Angus, latterly of Forbes, then Lochorwort and Yester在英國蘇格蘭費伏洛斯結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1466年在英國蘇格蘭易亞斯特 洛迪安可萊格雷斯享年74歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭哈丁頓史爾也斯特吉福德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir David Hay of Lochorwart and Yester于大约1394年出生在英国苏格兰米德洛迪安时，他的父亲Sir William Hay, Laird of Locherworth and Yester20岁，母亲Alice de la Haye13岁。他于1425年与Elizabeth Mary Douglas of Angus, latterly of Forbes, then Lochorwort and Yester在英国苏格兰費伏洛斯结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于大约1466年在英国苏格兰易亞斯特 洛迪安可萊格雷斯享年74岁，埋葬在苏格兰哈丁頓史爾也斯特吉福德。

nameMeanings.name: Hay
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and English (northern; of Norman origin): habitational name from any of several places in Normandy called La Haie or La Haye (Old French haie ‘hedge, enclosure’, ‘forest for hunting deer and other animals’, a borrowing of the ancient Germanic word haga). Robert de Haia or de la Haye is known to have come from La Haye-du-Puits in Manche; he was the founder of Boxgrove Priory in Sussex (1123), and holder of the Honor of Halnaker (Sussex) and (by marriage) the barony of Kolswein (Lincolnshire). The Norman name was also taken to Ireland, where it has since flourished in the county of Wexford as Hay and Hayes . Elsewhere in Ireland the name usually has a native Irish origin, see below.

English: topographic name from Middle English hay(e), heye, heghe ‘enclosure’ (Old English (ge)hæg) or ‘forest fenced off for hunting’ (Old French haie); or else a habitational name from a place so called, such as Hay (in Herefordshire and Westmorland) or Hey in Scammonden (Yorkshire). It was no doubt sometimes synonymous with Hayward .
English: nickname for a tall man, from Middle English heigh, hey, high ‘high, tall’ (Old English hēah).

variants.name: High
meanings: English:
nickname for a tall man, from Middle English heigh, hey, high ‘high, tall’ (Old English hēah). Compare Hay 3.
topographic name for someone who lived ‘(on the) height or hill’, from Middle English heigh ‘height, hilltop’ (Old English hēah). Compare Hight .

variants.name: Hai
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Abdul, Syed, Arifa, Atta, Faiz, Hamid, Iftekhar, Iskandar, Mohamed, Mohammad, Mohannad.  Vietnamese Mui, Pham, Tran, Vo, Bui, Cao, Chau, Dinh, Duong, Nguyen, Sau, Vo Van.  Jewish Ilan, Ronen, Shlomo.  Chinese Chan, Chiming, Lung, Wai Fun, Yin.
Muslim (mainly the Indian subcontinent): from a personal name based on Arabic ḥayy ‘alive’. Al-Ḥayy ‘the Living’ is an attribute of Allah.
Jewish: variant of   Hay  .

variants.name: Dehay
meanings: French: topographic name, with fused preposition de ‘from’, for someone who lived by an enclosure, from the masculine form of Old French haye ‘hedge’; or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word (see Hay 9).
Altered form (also De Hay) of Dutch De Haij or De Haaij, which is probably of French origin (see above).

variants.name: Haig
meanings: Scottish and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of various places in northern France named with Old Norse hagi ‘enclosure’, a word with cognates in most ancient Germanic languages. Compare Hay . The Haigs of Bemersyde in Berwickshire derive their name from the district of La Hague in Manche.
Scottish and Irish (Cavan): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Thaoig and Mac Thaidhg (see McCaig ).

variants.name: Hayman
meanings: English: topographic name probably from Middle English hai(e), hei(e) ‘enclosure’ (Old English (ge)hæg), denoting a dweller by an enclosure (see Hay 2 ), or else a nickname for a ‘keeper of the enclosure’ (compare Hayward ).
English: nickname for a tall man, from Middle English heigh, high(e) (Old English hēah); see also Hay 3.
English: occupational name for a seller of hay, from Middle English hai, hei (Old English hēg, hīg).

variants.name: Hayes
meanings: Irish (Cork): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hAodha ‘descendant of Aodh’, a personal name meaning ‘fire’. Compare McCoy . In some cases especially in County Wexford, the surname is of English origin (see below), having been taken to Ireland by the Normans.
English: variant of Hay , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

English: topographic name from the plural form of Middle English hay(e), heye, heghe ‘enclosure’ (see Hay ), sometimes used as a collective noun for a farm, especially in Devon, where it is a frequent minor placename. Compare Hain .

variants.name: Haycock
meanings: English: from a pet form of the Middle English personal name Hai + coc; see Hay 4 and compare Cocke .

variants.name: Haylett
meanings: English (Norfolk): from a pet form (double diminutive) of the personal name Hai (see Hay 4), from Hai + -el + -ot.

variants.name: Hey
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): variant of   Hay  .
Dutch: variant, mostly Americanized (or archaic) and Flemish, of Heij, a topographic name for someone who lived on a heath, Dutch hei, heide.
German: metonymic occupational name for a grower or mower of grass, from Middle High German höu ‘grass, hay’.

nameMeanings.name: David
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the greatest of all the kings of Israel, whose history is recounted with great vividness in the first and second books of Samuel and elsewhere. As a boy he killed the giant Philistine Goliath with his slingshot. As king of Judah, and later of all Israel, he expanded the power of the Israelites and established the security of their kingdom. He was also noted as a poet, many of the Psalms being attributed to him. The Hebrew derivation of the name is uncertain; it is said by some to represent a nursery word meaning ‘darling’. It is a very popular Jewish name, but is almost equally common among Gentiles in the English-speaking world. It is particularly common in Wales and Scotland, having been borne by the patron saint of Wales (  see  Dewi   ) and by two medieval kings of Scotland.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir David Hay of Lochorwart and Yester was born about 1394, in Midlothian, Scotland, United Kingdom, his father, Sir William Hay, Laird of Locherworth and Yester, was 20 and his mother, Alice de la Haye, was 13. He married Elizabeth Mary Douglas of Angus, latterly of Forbes, then Lochorwort and Yester in 1425, in Culross, Fife, Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. He died about 1466, in Craigleith, East Lothian, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 74, and was buried in Gifford, Yester, Haddingtonshire, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir David Hay of Lochorwart and Yester est né environ 1394, à Midlothian, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir William Hay, Laird of Locherworth and Yester, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Alice de la Haye, avait 13 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Mary Douglas of Angus, latterly of Forbes, then Lochorwort and Yester en 1425, à Culross, Fife, Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé environ 1466, à Craigleith, East Lothian, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 74 ans, et a été enterré à Gifford, Yester, Haddingtonshire, Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir David Hay of Lochorwart and Yester um 1394 in Midlothian, Schottland, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir William Hay, Laird of Locherworth and Yester 20 und seine Mutter Alice de la Haye 13. Er hat 1425 in Culross, Fife, Schottland, United Kingdom Elizabeth Mary Douglas of Angus, latterly of Forbes, then Lochorwort and Yester geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb um 1466 im Alter von 74 Jahren in Craigleith, East Lothian, Schottland, United Kingdom und wurde in Gifford, Yester, Haddingtonshire, Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir David Hay of Lochorwart and Yester è nato il circa 1394 nel Midlothian, Scozia, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Sir William Hay, Laird of Locherworth and Yester, aveva 20 anni e il suo madre, Alice de la Haye, aveva 13 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth Mary Douglas of Angus, latterly of Forbes, then Lochorwort and Yester nel 1425, nel Culross, Fife, Scozia, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Morì circa 1466, nel Craigleith, East Lothian, Scozia, Regno Unito, all'età di 74 anni, e fu sepolto a Gifford, Yester, Haddingtonshire, Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir David Hay of Lochorwart and Yesterが約1394年にイギリススコットランドミドロスアンで生まれた時，父Sir William Hay, Laird of Locherworth and Yesterは20歳，母Alice de la Hayeは13歳であった。1425年にイギリススコットランドフィフクウルロスでElizabeth Mary Douglas of Angus, latterly of Forbes, then Lochorwort and Yesterと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は約1466年にイギリススコットランドエアスト ロスアンクレイグレイスで74歳で死去し，スコットランドハッドイントンシュアイアイエスターギフォードにて埋葬された。

ko.Yester Castle, Yester, Haddingtonshire,: 영국 스코틀랜드 이스트 로스이언 예스터 예스터 카스틀
ko.Gifford, Yester, East Lothian, Scotland,: 영국 스코틀랜드 이스트 로스이언 예스터 지퍼드
ko.lifeSummary: Sir David Hay of Lochorwart and Yester(이)가 대략 1394년 영국 스코틀랜드 미들로스이언에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir William Hay, Laird of Locherworth and Yester(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice de la Haye(은)는 13세였다. 그는 1425년 영국 스코틀랜드 피프 컬로스에서 Elizabeth Mary Douglas of Angus, latterly of Forbes, then Lochorwort and Yester(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1466년 영국 스코틀랜드 이스트 로스이언 크레이글레이스에서 74세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 하딩토실르 예스터 지퍼드에 묻혔다.

pt.Yester Castle, Yester, Lothian, Scotland: Craigleith, East Lothian, Escócia, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir David Hay of Lochorwart and Yester nasceu aproximadamente 1394, em Midlothian, Escócia, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir William Hay, Laird of Locherworth and Yester, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Alice de la Haye, tinha 13 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Mary Douglas of Angus, latterly of Forbes, then Lochorwort and Yester em 1425, em Culross, Fife, Escócia, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1466, em Craigleith, East Lothian, Escócia, United Kingdom, com 74 anos, e foi sepultado em Gifford, Yester, Haddingtonshire, Scotland.

ru.Yester Castle, Yester, Haddingtonshire,: иестир кастл, иестир, эаст лотян, Шотландия, Великобритания
ru.Gifford, Yester, East Lothian, Scotland,: гиффорд, иестир, эаст лотян, Шотландия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir David Hay of Lochorwart and Yester родился около 1394 г. в местности мидлотян, Шотландия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir William Hay, Laird of Locherworth and Yester было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice de la Haye, 13. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Mary Douglas of Angus, latterly of Forbes, then Lochorwort and Yester в 1425 г. в местности кулросс, фиф, Шотландия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер около 1466 г. в местности креиглит, эаст лотян, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 74 лет и был похоронен в местности гиффорд, иестир, хаддингтоншир, Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir David Hay of Lochorwart and Yester nació aproximadamente 1394, en Midlothian, Escocia, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir William Hay, Laird of Locherworth and Yester, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Alice de la Haye, tenía 13 años. Se casó con Elizabeth Mary Douglas of Angus, latterly of Forbes, then Lochorwort and Yester en 1425, en Culross, Fife, Escocia, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió aproximadamente 1466, en Craigleith, East Lothian, Escocia, Reino Unido, a la edad de 74 años, y fue sepultado en Gifford, Yester, Haddingtonshire, Scotland.
